meta.originalTitle: Robert Louis Stevenson 苏格兰作家

children.text: You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 27–40, which are based on Reading Passage 3 below.
The writer of some of the best-known stories in the English language, including Treasure Island and The Strange Case of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde

It is more than 100 years since the death of the Scottish writer Robert Louis Stevenson on the South Pacific island of Samoa. And it seems that time has not been kind to Stevenson's memory. Immediately after his death, his family and friends set to work to fashion the legend of Robert Louis Stevenson, or R.L.S. as he became known—one of the few writers familiar from his initials alone. Subsequent works of biography then turned him into a writer of almost religious importance. One example was critic Balfour, who in 1901 portrayed Stevenson's family as ministering angels to the dying genius during his final illness. Similarly, the biographer Crouch absurdly overstated Stevenson's significance by placing him in the same company as those most revered names in English literature, Shakespeare and Keats. The reaction to this nonsense was a number of highly critical assessments of Stevenson's legacy in the 1920s.

Normally, the critical pendulum can be relied on to swing back again, but there are several aspects of Stevenson's work that have until recently acted against a more balanced appraisal. First is the allegation that Stevenson was a mere master of linguistic fireworks who lacked moral depth. Some critics accused him of being a literary charlatan, juggling words very prettily to strike effects that overawed an ignorant public and served to distract from the inadequacy of his ideas.

Then there has been a prejudice against the adventure story as the proper medium for deep moral seriousness, a prejudice which is still extremely influential today. It seems that we can accept that an adventure film can successfully express profound moral truths, but we reject the same idea for a book. The absurdity of this becomes apparent when we think of writers like Joseph Conrad and Graham Greene, but it is no use pretending that this bias against adventure stories is not part of our high culture. A further problem is that Stevenson has often not found favour in the land of his birth because his conservatism so often collides with the strong radical tradition in Scotland. His many escapist stories and preference for living abroad have led to accusations that he camouflaged Scotland's real problems. Lastly, the high adventure of Stevenson's own lifestyle has sometimes obscured his output. His globe-trotting, and above all the final phase of his life in Samoa, tended to make his own life a greater story than any he could devise. This was precisely what his friends feared would happen towards the end of his short life: his art might be overwhelmed by the drama of life in Samoa.

One consequence of this has been that Stevenson's influence on other writers has too often been neglected. The writer and poet Oscar Wilde was deeply influenced by Stevenson, even though he declared that Stevenson would have produced better work if he had lived in London rather than Samoa. Stevenson tends to stick in the throat even of those writers who would like to spit him out, such as Shaw, who claimed to have learned from him that the romantic hero is always mocked by reality. Likewise, the writer Galsworthy, who was a determined critic, later changed his mind and said that the superiority of Stevenson over the novelist Hardy was that Stevenson was all life and Hardy, all death. The influence on the novelist Chesterton would also repay detailed study, for it was through him that Stevenson has managed to cross the ages, emerging as an influence on the modernist movement and our own contemporary Latin American school of magical realism.

When making an assessment of his life and work, one question must inevitably be asked: was Robert Louis Stevenson Scotland's greatest writer of English prose? For most commentators this honour falls to Sir Walter Scott, author of Ivanhoe among many other classic novels, and it is true that in terms of craftsmanship, precision and the ability to minutely regulate language to create the desired effect, Scott takes the prize. However, this is not the same thing at all as inherent talent: by way of comparison one may take the example of the two great Russian composers Shostakovich and Prokofiev, of whom the former had learned more precise skills of execution but the latter's intrinsic genius was greater, and so it seems to be with Scott and Stevenson. Admittedly, Scott's detailed style does permit his stories to explore levels of tragedy that are beyond Stevenson's reach, but in this regard they have the musty smell of the museum, somehow artificial and removed from modern-day reality. On the other hand, Stevenson's skill with plotting and narrative give his books a timeless quality, so that they still live today. And Stevenson was also the shrewder judge of behaviour and psychology. For example, his compelling description of a man with a split personality in The Strange Case of Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde has proved so accessible and accurate that the expression “Jekyll and Hyde” has entered common English usage. Even if we do not see a revival of critical interest in this great Scottish writer, it is to be hoped that readers go back to Robert Louis Stevenson's magnificent stories and reassess this neglected genius.
In the opinion of the writer, the biographers Balfour and Crouch
B overestimated other writers' influence on Stevenson
C elevated Stevenson above his true status as a writer
D understated the role played by Stevenson's family
What is the writer's main point about Stevenson in the second paragraph?
A The ethical nature of his stories was often criticised.
B The public judges him more fairly than the critics.
C Recent criticism of him has been justified.
D Critics argued that his style covered up his faults.
According to the writer, the adventure story
A can be used by writers to tell moral stories
B is more fashionable today than in the past
C has been used by other writers but not Stevenson
D is more appropriate for books than for films
What point does the writer make about Stevenson and Scotland?
A His unflattering stories about Scotland angered many Scots.
B His ideas contrasted with those of many Scots.
C He demonstrated great sympathy for Scotland's problems.
D He was not considered a true Scot as he was not born there.
According to the writer, Stevenson's own lifestyle
A attracted more attention than his books
B did not prepare him for living in Samoa
C was envied by his friends
D was responsible for his early death

explanationSection.text:
答案：D（elevated Stevenson above his true status as a writer. 将史蒂文森的地位抬高于其真实水平）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “Balfour... portrayed Stevenson’s family as ministering angels to the dying genius... Crouch absurdly overstated Stevenson’s significance by placing him in the same company as... Shakespeare and Keats”，“神化家人”“与莎士比亚并列” 均体现 “夸大地位”，因此选 D。A（低估其家人作用）、B（误解其宗教信仰）、C（高估其他作家对他的影响）均与原文矛盾。
答案：C（Critics argued that his style covered up his faults. 批评者认为他的风格掩盖了自身缺陷）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “Some critics accused him of being a literary charlatan, of juggling words very prettily to strike effects which overawed an ignorant public, and served to distract from the inadequacy of his ideas”，“用华丽辞藻分散对思想空洞的关注” 对应 “style covered up his faults”，因此选 C。A（公众比批评者更公平）、B（近期批评合理）、D（故事的道德性常被批评）均未提及。
答案：B（can be used by writers to tell moral stories. 作家可借助冒险故事讲述道德主题）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “we can accept that an adventure film can successfully express profound moral truths, but we reject the same idea for a book. The absurdity of this becomes apparent when we think of writers like Joseph Conrad and Graham Greene”，通过 “电影与小说的双重标准” 及康拉德、格林的例子，暗示 “冒险小说也能表达道德”，因此选 B。A（书籍比电影更适合）、C（如今更流行）、D（其他作家使用但史蒂文森未用）均与原文矛盾。
答案：A（His ideas contrasted with those of many Scots. 他的观点与多数苏格兰人冲突）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “his conservatism so often collides with the strong radical tradition in Scotland”，“保守主义与激进传统冲突” 对应 “ideas contrasted with those of many Scots”，因此选 A。B（他同情苏格兰问题）、C（因非出生于苏格兰不被视为真正苏格兰人）、D（其丑化苏格兰的故事激怒民众）均与原文矛盾或未提及。
答案：C（attracted more attention than his books. 比他的作品更受关注）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “the high adventure of Stevenson’s own lifestyle has sometimes obscured his output, his life a greater story than any he could devise. This was precisely what his friends feared would happen... his art might be overwhelmed by the drama of life in Samoa”，“生活盖过作品”“艺术被生活光环掩盖” 对应 “attracted more attention than his books”，因此选 C。A（被朋友羡慕）、B（导致早逝）、D（未帮助他适应萨摩亚）均未提及。

items.text:
（1）题目 27：In the opinion of the writer, the biographers Balfour and Crouch（作者认为传记作家鲍尔弗与克劳奇如何？）
答案：D（elevated Stevenson above his true status as a writer. 将史蒂文森的地位抬高于其真实水平）
解析：定位 Paragraph 1 中 “Balfour... portrayed Stevenson’s family as ministering angels to the dying genius... Crouch absurdly overstated Stevenson’s significance by placing him in the same company as... Shakespeare and Keats”，“神化家人”“与莎士比亚并列” 均体现 “夸大地位”，因此选 D。A（低估其家人作用）、B（误解其宗教信仰）、C（高估其他作家对他的影响）均与原文矛盾。

（2）题目 28：What is the writer’s main point about Stevenson in the second paragraph?（作者在第二段中关于史蒂文森的主要观点是？）
答案：C（Critics argued that his style covered up his faults. 批评者认为他的风格掩盖了自身缺陷）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “Some critics accused him of being a literary charlatan, of juggling words very prettily to strike effects which overawed an ignorant public, and served to distract from the inadequacy of his ideas”，“用华丽辞藻分散对思想空洞的关注” 对应 “style covered up his faults”，因此选 C。A（公众比批评者更公平）、B（近期批评合理）、D（故事的道德性常被批评）均未提及。

（3）题目 29：According to the writer, the adventure story（作者认为冒险故事如何？）
答案：B（can be used by writers to tell moral stories. 作家可借助冒险故事讲述道德主题）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “we can accept that an adventure film can successfully express profound moral truths, but we reject the same idea for a book. The absurdity of this becomes apparent when we think of writers like Joseph Conrad and Graham Greene”，通过 “电影与小说的双重标准” 及康拉德、格林的例子，暗示 “冒险小说也能表达道德”，因此选 B。A（书籍比电影更适合）、C（如今更流行）、D（其他作家使用但史蒂文森未用）均与原文矛盾。

（4）题目 30：What point does the writer make about Stevenson and Scotland?（作者关于史蒂文森与苏格兰的观点是？）
答案：A（His ideas contrasted with those of many Scots. 他的观点与多数苏格兰人冲突）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “his conservatism so often collides with the strong radical tradition in Scotland”，“保守主义与激进传统冲突” 对应 “ideas contrasted with those of many Scots”，因此选 A。B（他同情苏格兰问题）、C（因非出生于苏格兰不被视为真正苏格兰人）、D（其丑化苏格兰的故事激怒民众）均与原文矛盾或未提及。

（5）题目 31：According to the writer, Stevenson’s own lifestyle（作者认为史蒂文森的个人生活如何？）
答案：C（attracted more attention than his books. 比他的作品更受关注）
解析：定位 Paragraph 2 中 “the high adventure of Stevenson’s own lifestyle has sometimes obscured his output, his life a greater story than any he could devise. This was precisely what his friends feared would happen... his art might be overwhelmed by the drama of life in Samoa”，“生活盖过作品”“艺术被生活光环掩盖” 对应 “attracted more attention than his books”，因此选 C。A（被朋友羡慕）、B（导致早逝）、D（未帮助他适应萨摩亚）均未提及。

children.text: Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 32–35 on your answer sheet, write:
if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this
Although Oscar Wilde admired Robert Louis Stevenson very much, he believed Stevenson could have written greater works.
Robert Louis Stevenson encouraged Oscar Wilde to start writing in the first place.
Galsworthy respected Hardy's works more than Stevenson's.
There is a need to study in detail Stevenson's influence on Chesterton.

explanationSection.text:
答案：YES（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “Oscar Wilde was deeply influenced by Stevenson, even though he declared that Stevenson would have produced better work if he had lived in London rather than Samoa”，“深受影响（admired）” 与 “本可写出更好作品” 均与题干描述一致，因此判定为 YES。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：Paragraph 3 仅提到 “王尔德深受史蒂文森影响”，未提及 “鼓励其开始写作”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
答案：NO（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “Galsworthy, who began as a determined critic, later changed his mind and said that the superiority of Stevenson over the novelist Hardy was that Stevenson was all life and Hardy all death”，“史蒂文森优于哈代” 与题干 “更尊重哈代” 描述矛盾，因此判定为 NO。
答案：YES（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “The influence on the novelist Chesterton would also repay detailed study”，“repay detailed study”（值得深入研究）对应 “More research is needed”，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 YES。

items.text:
（1）题目 32：Although Oscar Wilde admired Stevenson’s work, he believed Stevenson could have written something better.（尽管奥斯卡・王尔德欣赏史蒂文森的作品，但认为他本可写出更好的作品）
答案：YES（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “Oscar Wilde was deeply influenced by Stevenson, even though he declared that Stevenson would have produced better work if he had lived in London rather than Samoa”，“深受影响（admired）” 与 “本可写出更好作品” 均与题干描述一致，因此判定为 YES。

（2）题目 33：Stevenson encouraged Oscar Wilde to start writing.（史蒂文森鼓励奥斯卡・王尔德开始写作）
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：Paragraph 3 仅提到 “王尔德深受史蒂文森影响”，未提及 “鼓励其开始写作”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（3）题目 34：Galsworthy had greater respect for Hardy than Stevenson.（高尔斯华绥更尊重哈代而非史蒂文森）
答案：NO（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “Galsworthy, who began as a determined critic, later changed his mind and said that the superiority of Stevenson over the novelist Hardy was that Stevenson was all life and Hardy all death”，“史蒂文森优于哈代” 与题干 “更尊重哈代” 描述矛盾，因此判定为 NO。

（4）题目 35：More research is needed regarding Stevenson’s influence on Chesterton.（需更多研究史蒂文森对切斯特顿的影响）
答案：YES（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “The influence on the novelist Chesterton would also repay detailed study”，“repay detailed study”（值得深入研究）对应 “More research is needed”，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 YES。

children.text: Complete the summary using the list of words,
Sir Walter Scott and Robert Louis Stevenson
A lot of people believe that Sir Walter Scott and Robert Louis Stevenson are the most influential writers in the history of Scotland, but Sir Walter Scott is more proficient in
, especially in terms of tragedy, but many readers prefer Stevenson's
gave his works a unique expression of the Scottish people.

explanationSection.text:
答案：F（technical control，技术掌控力）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “in terms of craftsmanship, precision and the ability to minutely regulate language to create the desired effect, Scott takes the prize”，“工艺、精准度、语言调控能力” 对应 “technical control”，因此选 F。
答案：A（natural ability，天赋）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “this is not the same thing at all as inherent talent; by way of comparison... the former had learned more precise skills of execution but the latter’s intrinsic genius was greater, and so it seems to be with Scott and Stevenson”，“司各特技巧更精，史蒂文森天赋更高”，“intrinsic genius” 对应 “natural ability”，因此选 A。
答案：H（depth，深度）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “Scott’s detailed style does permit his stories to explore levels of tragedy that are beyond Stevenson’s reach”，“探索更深层次的悲剧” 对应 “more depth”，因此选 H。
答案：G（storytelling，叙事能力）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “Stevenson’s skill with plotting and narrative gives his books a timeless quality, so that they still live today”，“情节与叙事技巧” 对应 “storytelling”，因此选 G。
答案：I（human nature，人性）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “Stevenson was also the shrewder judge of behaviour and psychology. For example, his compelling descriptions of a man with a split personality... ‘Jekyll and Hyde’ has entered common English usage”，“对行为与心理的洞察” 对应 “understanding of human nature”，因此选 I。

items.text:
（1）题目 36：Scott had greater .（司各特有更强的。）
答案：F（technical control，技术掌控力）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “in terms of craftsmanship, precision and the ability to minutely regulate language to create the desired effect, Scott takes the prize”，“工艺、精准度、语言调控能力” 对应 “technical control”，因此选 F。

（2）题目 37：but Stevenson had more .（但史蒂文森有更强的。）
答案：A（natural ability，天赋）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “this is not the same thing at all as inherent talent; by way of comparison... the former had learned more precise skills of execution but the latter’s intrinsic genius was greater, and so it seems to be with Scott and Stevenson”，“司各特技巧更精，史蒂文森天赋更高”，“intrinsic genius” 对应 “natural ability”，因此选 A。

（3）题目 38：It is true that Scott’s books showed more \___\___ when it came to tragedy.（诚然，司各特的作品在悲剧方面展现出更强的_\___\__。）
答案：H（depth，深度）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “Scott’s detailed style does permit his stories to explore levels of tragedy that are beyond Stevenson’s reach”，“探索更深层次的悲剧” 对应 “more depth”，因此选 H。

（4）题目 39：while Stevenson’s books are still popular because of his \___\___.（而史蒂文森的作品因他的_\___\__至今仍受欢迎。）
答案：G（storytelling，叙事能力）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “Stevenson’s skill with plotting and narrative gives his books a timeless quality, so that they still live today”，“情节与叙事技巧” 对应 “storytelling”，因此选 G。

（5）题目 40：And Stevenson’s understanding of \___\___ has resulted in the widespread use of an expression from one of his books.（史蒂文森对_\___\__的理解，使其作品中的一个表达被广泛使用。）
答案：I（human nature，人性）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “Stevenson was also the shrewder judge of behaviour and psychology. For example, his compelling descriptions of a man with a split personality... ‘Jekyll and Hyde’ has entered common English usage”，“对行为与心理的洞察” 对应 “understanding of human nature”，因此选 I。
